label: RSF Assault on Presidential Palace
notes: RSF assault route from Omdurman toward Khartoum Presidential Palace on April 15, 2023

label: RSF Cross-Nile Push: Omdurman to Khartoum
notes: RSF forces crossed the White Nile bridges from Omdurman into central Khartoum in the opening assault

label: RSF Darfur Expansion: Geneina → Nyala
notes: RSF extended control from West Darfur capital El Geneina eastward across Darfur to Nyala in South Darfur, Oct 2023

label: RSF Advance: South Khartoum → Wad Madani
notes: RSF advanced southward from Khartoum along the Blue Nile toward Gezira State, capturing Wad Madani on Dec 19, 2023

label: RSF Advance: Geneina → Zalingei
notes: RSF extended control from West Darfur into Central Darfur, capturing Zalingei (Central Darfur capital)

label: RSF Siege Drive: Nyala → El Fasher
notes: RSF advanced north from Nyala to encircle El Fasher, the last SAF-held Darfur capital, beginning 18-month siege

label: SAF Counteroffensive: Bahri → West Omdurman
notes: SAF counteroffensive from Bahri (Khartoum North) driving RSF out of western Omdurman, Sept 2024–Jan 2025

label: SAF Recapture: East Khartoum → City Center
notes: SAF advance from eastern Khartoum through the city center toward the Presidential Palace, Jan–Mar 2025

label: SAF Advance: Sennar → Wad Madani (Recapture)
notes: SAF forces advanced from Sennar north to recapture Wad Madani in January 2025, expelling RSF from Gezira State capital

label: SAF Relief Corridor: North → El Obeid
notes: SAF relief corridor that broke the RSF siege of El Obeid (Al-Ubayyid), North Kordofan capital, February 2025

label: Refugee Route: Khartoum → Port Sudan
notes: Primary displacement route for Khartoum civilians fleeing east to Port Sudan and beyond; millions used this corridor

label: Refugee Flow: Darfur → Chad (via Adre)
notes: Hundreds of thousands of Darfur refugees fled west into Chad via the Adre border crossing, creating a massive humanitarian crisis

label: Refugee Route: Khartoum → South Sudan (via Renk)
notes: Significant refugee flow south from Khartoum through Kordofan and White Nile State to Renk, South Sudan entry point

label: RSF Supply: Chad → Darfur (UAE-backed)
notes: UN sanctions monitors documented RSF weapons and supplies transiting through Chad, reportedly with UAE financial backing, into Darfur

label: SAF Supply: Egypt → Port Sudan
notes: Egyptian military support — drones, weapons, and supplies — entered Sudan through the Egyptian-controlled northern border and Port Sudan

label: SAF Logistics: Port Sudan → Khartoum Front
notes: Primary SAF supply route from wartime capital Port Sudan through Atbara and Shendi to the Khartoum front

label: RSF Push South: Wad Madani → Sennar
notes: After capturing Wad Madani, RSF advanced further south toward Sennar, threatening Blue Nile State and the Roseires Dam area

label: RSF Corridor: Darfur → Kordofan
notes: RSF established a corridor through North Kordofan linking Darfur to its forces besieging El Obeid and threatening central Sudan

label: SAF Airstrike Corridor: Khartoum → Omdurman RSF Positions
notes: SAF conducted sustained airstrikes from east to west across RSF positions in Omdurman, causing significant civilian casualties throughout 2023–2024

label: Humanitarian Aid Route: Adre → El Fasher
notes: Cross-border humanitarian corridor from Adre (Chad border) to El Fasher; frequently blocked by both SAF and RSF, severely limiting aid delivery

label: RSF Push: South Kordofan → El Obeid
notes: RSF advanced from South Kordofan northward, encircling El Obeid and cutting SAF ground supply routes to North Kordofan capital

label: Refugee Flow: Nyala → Chad (Tine Crossing)
notes: Following RSF capture of Nyala in October 2023, hundreds of thousands of South Darfur civilians fled westward into Chad via the Tine border crossing

label: SAF West Khartoum Push (2025)
notes: SAF advanced westward through Khartoum toward RSF holdouts; combined with Omdurman recapture to squeeze RSF from multiple directions

label: RSF Assault on Zamzam IDP Camp
notes: RSF forces assaulted Zamzam IDP camp (500,000 residents) from surrounding positions in April 2025, killing 300–1,500 civilians

label: Iranian Drone Supply to SAF (via Port Sudan)
notes: Iran supplied SAF with drone technology; Iranian-supplied Shahed-type drones reportedly used in SAF counteroffensive strikes across Khartoum and RSF positions

label: RSF Advance: East Darfur → El Fasher
notes: RSF advance from Al Daein (East Darfur) northwest toward El Fasher to tighten the siege from multiple directions

label: RSF Attack on Civilian Convoy → Ardamata
notes: RSF opened fire on a kilometers-long civilian evacuation convoy near El Geneina, pursuing survivors to the Kajja river and into Ardamata neighborhood

label: Refugee Route: Khartoum → Egypt (via Wadi Halfa)
notes: Major refugee route from Khartoum north through the Nile Valley to Wadi Halfa and into Egypt; over 700,000 Sudanese sought refuge in Egypt

label: SAF Advance: El Obeid → South Kordofan
notes: Following relief of El Obeid, SAF pushed southward into South Kordofan to contest RSF's Darfur–Kordofan corridor (ongoing as of early 2026)

label: Turkish Drone Supply to SAF
notes: Turkey supplied Bayraktar TB2 drones to SAF for use against RSF. Turkish-supplied drones were used in multiple airstrikes on RSF positions across Sudan.

label: RSF Gold Route: Jebel Amer → RSF Command
notes: RSF extracted gold from Jebel Amer mines and smuggled it predominantly to the UAE, generating estimated $860M/year to fund RSF operations

label: RSF-SPLM-N Advance: Ethiopia Border → Kurmuk
notes: RSF and SPLM-N (al-Hilu faction) captured Kurmuk on March 23, 2026, staging from Ethiopia's Benishangul-Gumuz region. ~3,000 fighters; SAF 16th Brigade withdrew. Sudan accused Ethiopia of facilitating the assault.

label: RSF-SPLM-N Push: Kurmuk → Ed Damazin (Roseires Dam threatened)
notes: After Kurmuk's fall, RSF-SPLM-N forces pushed north toward Ed Damazin and west toward Geissan, threatening the Roseires Dam and state capital. 73,000+ civilians fled.

label: SAF Drone Strikes: Port Sudan Area → Lagawa (March 23 & 27, 2026)
notes: SAF struck Lagawa, West Kordofan twice in March 2026: March 23 (15 killed, 23 wounded) and March 27 (15 killed). Residential neighborhoods and civilian infrastructure targeted. Total 30+ killed.

label: RSF Long-Range Drone Strike: Kordofan → Ad Dabbah (March 21, 2026)
notes: RSF drone struck Ad Dabbah in Northern State, killing 6 and damaging power infrastructure. Demonstrated RSF extended drone range into deep SAF-controlled northern Sudan territory.

label: RSF Recapture: South → Bara (March 16, 2026)
notes: RSF recaptured Bara on March 16, 2026, reversing earlier SAF gains and tightening pressure on El Obeid. Part of RSF counter-operations to restore the Kordofan corridor.

label: SAF Drone Strike: Funeral in Al Sunut, W. Kordofan (March 27, 2026)
notes: SAF drone struck a funeral in Al Sunut, West Kordofan, killing 7 and wounding 39. RSF-held area. Town previously struck on March 10 (50 killed).

label: SAF Drone Strike: Zalingei IDP Camp Area, Central Darfur (March 27-28, 2026)
damage: Residential buildings and Khamsa Daqaiq IDP camp area hit
notes: SAF drones struck Zalingei (RSF-occupied Central Darfur capital) on March 27-28, hitting residential buildings in the eastern district and parts of the Khamsa Daqaiq IDP camp. RSF civil administration condemned the strikes.

label: RSF-SPLM-N Assault on Dilling, South Kordofan (March 28-29, 2026)
notes: RSF and SPLM-N (al-Hilu) launched a major ground assault on Dilling, South Kordofan, on March 28, supported by artillery, drones, and South Sudanese mercenaries. Al-Tukma (7 km east) seized; Habila road cut. 14 civilians killed (5 children), 23 wounded by March 29. Sudan Doctors Network warned of El Fasher-scale catastrophe.

label: RSF-SPLM-N Pressure: Kurmuk → Geissan (March 30, 2026)
notes: After Kurmuk fell on March 23, RSF-SPLM-N massed forces toward Geissan (Qaysan) in Blue Nile State by March 30. Fall of Geissan would threaten Ed Damazin and the Roseires Dam. 1,645+ civilians displaced from Qaysan as of March 30.

label: SAF Drone Strikes: Port Sudan Area → Dilling Area (March 30, 2026)
damage: RSF-SPLM-N positions on mountain range west of Dilling targeted
notes: SAF drones continued striking RSF-SPLM-N positions on the mountain range west of Dilling on March 30 as the RSF-SPLM-N siege entered its third day. Ground access to the city remained severed.

label: SAF Drone Strike: Civilian Vehicle in Al-Rahad, N. Kordofan (March 26, 2026)
notes: Drone struck a civilian vehicle in Al-Rahad, North Kordofan, heading toward Um Rawaba. Part of the double-strike day that killed 28 total across Al-Rahad and Sarf Umra.

label: SAF Targeted Drone Strike: RSF Ta'asees Politician Killed, Nyala (April 1, 2026)
damage: House in Nyala Khartoum district hit; RSF parallel administration building struck
casualties: 1 killed (Osama Hassan, Ta'asees founder), 4 wounded; RSF 'PM' Hassan Al Taishy narrowly escaped
notes: SAF targeted killing of RSF-aligned Ta'asees coalition founder Osama Hassan in Nyala. Two munitions fired. Near-miss on RSF parallel 'Prime Minister' Al Taishy. Demonstrates SAF precision strike reach deep into RSF-controlled South Darfur.

label: RSF Multi-Front Assault on Dalang, South Kordofan (April 2, 2026)
notes: RSF-SPLM-N launched a multi-front ground assault on Dalang in the Nuba Mountains on April 2, briefly penetrating outer neighborhoods before SAF repelled the attack. Tribal tensions within RSF ranks (Arab recruits reluctant to fight Nuba fighters) weakened assault cohesion. Part of coordinated multi-front pressure on South Kordofan.

label: RSF Drone Strikes: Al-Jabalain Hospital, White Nile State (April 3, 2026)
damage: Operating theater and maternity ward directly struck; hospital severely damaged
notes: RSF launched two drone strikes on Al-Jabalain Hospital in SAF-controlled White Nile State, hitting the operating theater and maternity ward during a children's immunization campaign. Seven medical staff killed. RSF denied responsibility. Injured transferred ~80 km to Kosti hospital. Attack confirms RSF long-range drone capability against SAF rear areas.

label: RSF Drone Strike: Rabak Medical Supply Depot, White Nile State (April 3-4, 2026)
damage: Medical supply depot struck; healthcare supply chain degraded
notes: RSF drone struck a medical supply depot in Rabak, capital of White Nile State, concurrent with the Al-Jabalain Hospital attack. MSF disclosed the Rabak depot strike in its April 4 condemnation statement. Part of a coordinated RSF campaign against healthcare infrastructure in SAF-controlled White Nile State.

label: RSF-SPLM-N Artillery: Dilling Residential Areas, South Kordofan (April 5, 2026)
damage: Residential areas struck; civilian infrastructure damaged
notes: RSF and allied SPLM-N forces continued shelling residential areas of Dilling, South Kordofan on April 5. At least 5 civilians killed, 33 injured including children. Siege of the city continues; roads to surrounding areas cut off. Sudan Doctors Network warned of escalating humanitarian crisis.
